title: Customers Ordering All Products
description: Find customers who have ordered every product available in the products table.
sql_solution: SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM orders o1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM products p WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o2 WHERE o2.customer_id = o1.customer_id AND o2.product_id = p.id));
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *

customer_ids = orders.select("customer_id").distinct()

missing_products = (
    customer_ids.alias("c")
    .crossJoin(products.alias("p"))
    .join(
        orders.alias("o"),
        (col("c.customer_id") == col("o.customer_id")) & (col("p.id") == col("o.product_id")),
        "left_anti"
    )
    .select(col("c.customer_id").alias("customer_id"))
    .distinct()
)

result = (
    customer_ids.alias("c")
    .join(missing_products.alias("m"), col("c.customer_id") == col("m.customer_id"), "left_anti")
    .select(col("c.customer_id").alias("customer_id"))
)
